task_completion.description: Verify that the FZK-Haus model is loaded and visible in Blender/Bonsai.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blender is open with the FZK-Haus residential building model visible in the 3D viewport or the outliner.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least three new 3D mesh objects representing clearances have been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least three new 3D mesh objects (e.g., circles, cylinders, or empty spaces representing clearances) are visible in the 3D viewport or outliner.

task_completion.description: Verify that the new objects are assigned the IFC class 'IfcVirtualElement'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bonsai/BlenderBIM Object Properties panel shows the IFC class 'IfcVirtualElement' assigned to the newly created objects.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Name attribute of the new objects includes the required keywords.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The outliner or object properties show the new objects have names containing at least one of the following: 'Clearance', 'Turning', 'Wheelchair', or 'Access'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the virtual elements are spatially contained within the Ground Floor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The outliner hierarchy shows the new virtual elements nested under the Ground Floor building storey (IfcBuildingStorey), or the Bonsai spatial containment panel indicates they belong to the Ground Floor.

task_completion.description: Verify that the modified project is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, Blender info notification, or file manager shows the project being saved to '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_accessibility.ifc'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the Blender/Bonsai GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the Blender/Bonsai interface to create objects and assign IFC properties, rather than using a terminal or Python console to script the changes.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent created new objects rather than modifying existing structural elements.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent creates new mesh objects for the clearances rather than just renaming or reclassifying existing walls or doors in the model.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not manually edit the IFC file in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent does not open the IFC file in a text editor to manually insert the required elements and keywords.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating at least 3 virtual elements with names containing 'Clearance', 'Turning', 'Wheelchair', or 'Access'. The elements must be assigned the class 'IfcVirtualElement' and spatially contained within the Ground Floor. The final file must be saved to /home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_accessibility.ifc. Note: The original model must remain intact (at least 10 original walls must be preserved).
